recalls.Description: This recall involves all Lifepro Bioremedy Infrared Sauna Blankets with model numbers LP-BRMDYL-BLK, LP-BRMDYL-GRY, LP-BRMDYR-BLK, LP-BRMDYR-GRY, LP-BRMDYR-BLU, LP-BRMDYR-PNK, and LP-BRMDYR-PRPL. Only blankets with a control pad that has a gray face are included in this recall. The recalled sauna blankets were sold in two sizes, 31 inches by 71 inches (regular) and 36 inches by 76 inches (large). The sauna blankets come in black, gray, pink and purple. "Lifepro" and the model number are printed on the front of the blanket and the controller.
recalls.Title: Lifepro Fitness Recalls Bioremedy Infrared Sauna Blankets Due to Risk of Burn Injuries
recalls.ConsumerContact: Lifepro toll-free at 888-491-1690 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, by email at recalls@lifeprofitness.com, or online https://lifeprofitness.com/recall or https://lifeprofitness.com and click on "Recall" on the page for more information.

Images.Caption: Controller Pad on Recalled Lifepro Sauna Blanket

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 65 reports of the sauna blankets overheating, including 32 reports of burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Online only at Lifepro.com and Amazon.com, Walmart, QVC and Dick's Sporting Goods from September 2022 through June 2025 for between $179 and $199.

Hazards.Name: The sauna blankets can overheat, posing a burn hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately stop using and unplug the recalled sauna blanket and go to the firm's website for instructions on how to participate in the recall and obtain a replacement sauna blanket. Consumers should dispose of the recalled sauna blankets in a manner consistent with local disposal regulations.

recalls.Description: This recall involves MaxKare Electric Blankets with six heating levels and a nine-hour auto-off function. They were sold in three sizes: 62 inches by 84 inches (twin), 72 inches by 84 inches (full), and 84 inches by 90 inches (queen). The recall only includes model numbers HB18A-7284-1, HB18A-8490-2 and HB18A-6284-1. The brand name, MaxKare, and the model numbers are on the tag attached to the blanket. The blankets come in brown and gray and are machine washable.
recalls.Title: MaxKare Electric Blankets Recalled Due to Burn and Fire Hazards; Manufactured by Yumo and Sold Exclusively on Walmart.com
recalls.ConsumerContact: Yumo toll-free at 866-620-0026 from 8 a.m. to 11:30 a.m. ET and 8 p.m. to 11:30 p.m. Monday through Thursday, online at https://www.marnur.net/pages/important-recall-information or www.marnur.net and click on "Important Recall Information" for more informationt.

Images.Caption: Recalled MaxKare Electric Blanket (Model HB18A/ Brown)
Recalled MaxKare Electric Blanket (Model HB18A/ Gray)
Recalled MaxKare Electric Blanket (Model HB18A Gray)
Recalled MaxKare Electric Blanket (Model HB18A Brown)
Recalled MaxKare Model HB18A Electric Blanket Controller

Injuries.Name: Yumo has received 34 reports of overheating, including three reports of burn injuries and two reports that the blankets caught on fire.

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhen Yumo Commerce and Trade Corporation, of China

Retailers.Name: Exclusively on Amazon from September 2024 through May 2025 for between $12 and $47.

Hazards.Name: The recalled blankets can overheat and ignite, posing burn and fire hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled blankets and contact Yumo for instructions on how to properly dispose of the blankets. Consumers will be asked to upload a photo of the blanket to yumoofficial@126.com and confirm disposal in order to get a full refund.
